cataloging_subject.label: A Volume of English Miscellanies Illustrating the History and Language of the Northern Counties of England: CME00131 documentary selection
cataloging_subject.scope_note: A composite, discontinuous selection from James Raine Jr.'s 1890 modern documentary miscellany, represented by five top-level encoded groups: section I complete (pp. 1–10), section II complete (pp. 11–22), only pp. 22–29 of section III, section IV complete (pp. 35–52), and section VI complete as a printed section (pp. 58–63), plus the edition title page. The selection is not one medieval document, not one manuscript, and not the whole 1890 carrier: it omits section III pp. 30–34, section V pp. 53–57, all of section VII, the glossary appendix, the index, and other preliminaries, paratext, and apparatus. The medieval documentary groups, archival witnesses, Raine's edition, U-M encoding, repository XML, and Internet Archive scan remain distinct lineage layers.

catalog_summary.title: A Volume of English Miscellanies Illustrating the History and Language of the Northern Counties of England: CME00131 documentary selection

catalog_summary.date: Historical documents 1417–1501, with the New Malton customs dated broadly to the fifteenth century; edited miscellany published 1890
catalog_summary.region: York, Selby, and New Malton in Yorkshire and northern England

agents.name: Named and anonymous civic, manorial, certificate, and borough documentary creators
notes: A cataloging collective for the many mayors, searchers, craftspeople, jurors, clerks, certificate issuers, burgesses, and other participants whose records are edited here; it does not claim joint authorship, one institution, or one uniform creator role.

agents.name: James Raine Jr.
notes: 1830–1896; editor, compiler, transcriber, commentator, and Surtees Society Secretary. He is not the author of the medieval documentary records.

scope.description: Historical documentary creation across all five encoded groups

notes: The schema role author is used broadly for documentary creation. At item level, more precise roles may include issuer, declarant, juror, searcher, clerk, petitioner, witness, or institutional authority; named and anonymous responsibilities should be separated when a full item crosswalk exists.

language_statements.label: English

notes: Modern editorial English is distinct from the Middle English documentary text. This facet covers only editorial passages retained by CME00131, not Raine's unencoded preface, glossary apparatus, index, or other omitted carrier matter.

form_statements.description: Five prose documentary groups comprising proceedings, verdicts, court presentments, certificates or letters, and borough customs.

scope.description: York civic proceedings, building verdicts, and enrolled certificates

subject_statements.label: Building survey, property boundaries, and urban drainage

summaries.value: This composite selection brings together five distinct groups of late medieval northern English records. A 1428 York proceeding investigates adulterated iron, tin, and other trade goods; twenty-one York searchers' verdicts regulate encroachments, boundaries, gutters, walls, and building obligations from 1417 to 1501; eight selected Selby manorial jury presentments report local offences and obligations in the 1470s and 1480s; twenty-one York-enrolled certificates address the English parentage and birth of people accused of being Scots from 1476 to 1497; and a fifteenth-century text sets out the customs and liberties of New Malton burgesses. Raine's modern editorial selection is valuable for civic government, craft regulation, urban property, manorial jurisdiction, identity, documentary practice, and borough custom, but it does not make these records one medieval work.

CME00131 contains the 1890 edition title page and five top-level body groups: section I, one account on pp. 1–10; section II, twenty-one editorial document containers on pp. 11–22; the first eight containers of section III on pp. 22–29; section IV, twenty-one containers on pp. 35–52; and section VI, one customs text on pp. 58–63. Section IV pp. 51–52 is textually present after PB 50 even though PB 51 and PB 52 are absent. The encoding omits section III pp. 30–34, section V pp. 53–57, all of section VII, the glossary appendix, the index, and other preliminaries, paratext, and apparatus.

This record catalogs the five-group CME documentary selection as a composite unit inherited from Raine's modern miscellany. It does not assign James Raine Jr. authorship of the historical records, treat the selection as one manuscript, count the seven carrier sections as encoded extent, or infer exact manuscript components from legacy locators and dates. The five top-level groups are the primary extent units. Their 50 nested DIV3 containers and two directly encoded prose groups yield 52 edition/encoding terminal units, but these are not a verified count of independent physical documents. Each group therefore carries its own date, place, language, witness, and completeness scope.

extent.description: Five encoded top-level documentary groups, corresponding to carrier sections I, II, partial III, IV, and VI, all in prose. They contain one direct prose unit in I, twenty-one nested editorial units in II, eight of the carrier section III units, twenty-one nested editorial units in IV, and one direct prose unit in VI. The resulting 52 terminal encoding units are editorial/structural metrics, not a physical-document count. The unencoded carrier sections V and VII are not counted, nor are the glossary appendix, index, or other paratext.

content_parts.label: I. Proceedings in a Case of Adulteration at York
content_parts.summary: A 1428 York civic investigation and adjudication concerning false osmund iron, adulterated tin and alum, craft searchers, trade fraud, and the punishment and negotiated restoration of John Lyllyng.
notes: Complete relative to carrier section I. The relation between legacy A.Y. 255 and modern fols. 264v.36–267v.6 remains a locator-system question.

content_parts.label: II. Verdicts of the Searchers in the City of York
content_parts.summary: Twenty-one editorial document containers of verdicts and awards by York craft searchers concerning building lines, walls, gutters, drainage, privies, encroachments, fixtures, and shared property obligations from 1417 to 1501.
notes: Complete relative to carrier section II. item_count and metrics count this top-level encoded documentary group as one extent unit; its twenty-one nested containers are Raine/U-M editorial units, not a verified physical-document count.

content_parts.label: III. Presentments at the Courts of the Abbot of Selby, encoded portion
content_parts.summary: Eight encoded editorial units of jury presentments associated with the Selby manorial court rolls, dated or assigned approximately 1472–1483, concerning local offences, customary duties, maintenance, and community regulation.

locators.value: U DDLO/21/27–30, /32, /35, and /40; exact mapping unresolved

notes: Partial relative to carrier section III: CME stops cleanly at the bottom of printed p. 29 and omits pp. 30–34. item_count and metrics count this partial top-level group as one extent unit; the eight nested containers remain described in the summary. The candidate roll children are date-compatible only and are not asserted one-to-one matches.

content_parts.label: IV. Certificates of English Parentage and Birth
content_parts.summary: Twenty-one editorial containers of certificates, letters, attestations, and related records documenting the English birth and parentage of people charged with being Scots, dated 1476–1497 and enrolled in York civic records.
notes: Complete relative to carrier section IV. item_count and metrics count this top-level encoded documentary group as one extent unit; its twenty-one nested containers remain described in the summary. Printed pp. 51–52 are textually present after PB 50, although the encoding has no PB 51 or PB 52; the phrase/content boundary, not the PB count, establishes completeness.

content_parts.label: VI. Customs and Liberties of the Burgesses of New Malton
content_parts.summary: A fifteenth-century prose text setting out offices, procedures, customs, obligations, and liberties of the New Malton burgesses.

locators.value: original roll in the possession of Earl Fitzwilliam

notes: Complete relative to carrier section VI. Completeness relative to the original roll is unknown, and the witness's current location and survival are unresolved.

related_works.title: A Volume of English Miscellanies Illustrating the History and Language of the Northern Counties of England
notes: The complete carrier has seven numbered sections, a glossary appendix, and an index. CME00131 is the exact discontinuous subset described in the cataloging scope, not a complete representation of this related carrier work.

evidence.locator: TEMPHEAD; IDG; FRONT; BODY; DIV2 N=1, 2, 3, 4, and 6; PB and phrase boundaries
evidence.summary: Supplies the encoded title and identifier, five documentary groups, headings, historical dates, source locators, languages visible in the text, structural units, page milestones, notes, and 2016 review history. Phrase comparison establishes inclusion of section IV pp. 51–52 despite absent PB milestones.

evidence.citation: Element, page-sequence, and source-boundary inspection of repository CME00131.xml against the complete 1890 carrier.
evidence.locator: FRONT/DIV1; BODY/DIV1/DIV2; DIV3; HEAD; HEADNOTE; P; NOTE; PB; TABLE
evidence.summary: Counts 2 DIV1, 5 DIV2, 50 DIV3, 52 PB, 14 HEAD, 5 HEADNOTE, 157 P, 33 NOTE, and one TABLE. Confirms five top-level prose groups, their 1/21/8/21/1 terminal-unit structure, exact printed selection title page + pp. 1–29 + 35–52 + 58–63, the section III cutoff, and the section IV PB anomaly.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan, Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse, CME00131 item and collection pages.
evidence.summary: Confirms the public electronic item, stable identifier, title and edition statement, subject access, and U-M public-domain assertion.

evidence.citation: James Raine Jr., ed., A Volume of English Miscellanies Illustrating the History and Language of the Northern Counties of England, Surtees Society 85 (1890).
evidence.summary: The complete carrier establishes title, editor/compiler context, publication details, preface source statements, all seven sections and apparatus, exact pagination and selection boundaries, documentary contents and dates, languages, and legacy source locators.

evidence.citation: Folger Shakespeare Library catalog record 49702, A Volume of English Miscellanies.
evidence.summary: Confirms the 1890 publication, ix and 100-page extent, and James Raine's editorial responsibility.

evidence.citation: Explore York Y/COU/1/4/1, York Memorandum Book A.
evidence.summary: Supports the current volume-level archival identity and holding for Raine's A/Y source.

evidence.citation: Explore York Y/COU/1/4/2, York Memorandum Book B.
evidence.summary: Supports the current volume-level identity and holding for Raine's B/Y source without creating a separate 'Enrolment Book' witness.

evidence.citation: Jinming Yi, A Study of York's Civic Administrative Literacy: Writing, Records and Archives, 1272–1377 (PhD thesis, University of York, 2022).
evidence.summary: Explicitly maps Y/COU/1/4/1 to York Memorandum Book A/Y and Y/COU/1/4/2 to York Memorandum Book B/Y; the complete licensed thesis PDF is cached and modeled in lineage.

evidence.citation: Middle English Grammar Corpus, text catalog entry for the York adulteration case.
evidence.summary: Links the adulteration text to York Memorandum Book A/Y 255 and fols. 264v.36–267v.6, preserving rather than silently reconciling the two locator forms; automated caching was blocked by a dated HTTP 403.

evidence.citation: Explore York Y/COU/1/1, York Corporation House Books.
evidence.summary: Supports the current aggregate House Books series for selected sections II and IV records; no direct numeric mapping from Raine's Roman-numbered volumes to current children is inferred.

evidence.citation: Hull History Centre U DDLO/21 and complete U DDLO catalog, Manor of Selby cum Membris court rolls.
evidence.summary: Supports the aggregate present archival context for Raine's Selby jury presentments and identifies date-compatible child candidates while leaving exact item and membrane matching unresolved.

evidence.citation: North Yorkshire County Record Office, Fitzwilliam Family of Malton, 23 June 2020.
evidence.summary: Shows that the current ZPB Fitzwilliam archive begins in 1593 and therefore does not identify the fifteenth-century roll; Raine's separate edition evidence supplies the 1890 Earl Fitzwilliam provenance.

open_questions.question: How do the York records in the five-group selection map from Raine's A/Y, B/Y, and Roman-numbered House Book locators to current foliation and child shelfmarks?
next_steps: Obtain Explore York legacy finding aids and concordances.
Match incipits and explicit Raine locators against complete volume images.
Create a passage-level crosswalk preserving both locator systems and the A.Y. 255 versus fols. 264v.36–267v.6 discrepancy.

open_questions.question: Which U DDLO/21 components and membranes contain the eight encoded Selby presentments, and what are their verified dates?
next_steps: Inspect complete images and endorsements for candidate children U DDLO/21/27–30, /32, /35, and /40.
Match incipits, jury places, regnal dates, and membrane order rather than relying only on marginal dates.
Preserve edition date inconsistencies until manuscript evidence resolves them.

open_questions.question: What is the current repository, shelfmark, survival status, physical extent, and textual completeness of the New Malton customs roll reported in Earl Fitzwilliam's possession in 1890?
next_steps: Contact North Yorkshire Archives, the Fitzwilliam (Malton) Estate, and repositories holding Wentworth-Fitzwilliam papers with Raine's exact title, incipit, and provenance.
Search deposit, transfer, and sale records without assuming ZPB contains the roll.
If located, request the complete object and compare it with Raine pp. 58–63 before calling the edition transcription complete.

open_questions.question: What production rationale explains the omission of section III pp. 30–34 and all of section V, and the retention of section IV pp. 51–52 without PB milestones?
next_steps: Inspect U-M keying specifications, proofsheets, and production logs.
Retain the current phrase-based selection boundary and do not restore text or manufacture PB milestones without provenance.
Record any future restoration or PB repair as a new encoding intervention.

notes: The five content parts, not the seven carrier sections, define encoded extent. Section III is partial; sections I, II, IV, and VI are complete relative to their printed section boundaries.
The 50 DIV3 containers plus the two directly encoded prose groups are editorial and encoding units, not a verified total of independent medieval documents or physical objects.
James Raine Jr. is recorded as editor, compiler, transcriber, and commentator of the 1890 miscellany, never as author of its medieval civic, manorial, certificate, or borough records.
Section IV printed pp. 51–52 is present after PB 50 even though the XML lacks PB 51 and PB 52. Completeness is based on phrase/content comparison with the carrier, not milestone count alone.

No complete public facsimile was found for the York, Selby, or New Malton manuscript sources on 2026-07-13. The cached Internet Archive PDF is a complete scan of Raine's printed carrier, not of the manuscripts.

The research exposed no reusable modeling lesson beyond the existing skill guidance on separating layers, caching complete carrier objects, preserving legacy/current locator uncertainty, and comparing phrase boundaries when milestone sequences are incomplete; no shared skill file was edited by this research worktree.
